title: crotalaria bojeri
intro: Crotalaria bojeri is a plant species of the Fabaceae family, commonly known as a legume or a pea-flower plant. It is native to Madagascar and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant contains bioactive compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, Crotalaria bojeri is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
